narratives.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narratives.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
narratives.reasoning: The text criticizes Al Gore, a prominent figure in the climate movement, for his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuels despite advocating for their reduction.

narratives.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
narratives.reasoning: The text downplays the significance of climate change by pointing out that past predictions of catastrophic events have not come true, implying that current warnings are also exaggerated.

narratives.narrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science
narratives.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
narratives.reasoning: The text questions the accuracy of climate science by highlighting the alleged failure of past predictions, implying that the science is flawed or unreliable.

narratives.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
narratives.reasoning: The text criticizes the climate movement for using sensational and frightening language to describe weather events, implying that this is a tactic to manipulate public opinion.

narratives.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for “renewable energy,” meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
narratives.reasoning: The text expresses skepticism about the benefits of renewable energy technologies, implying that they are not a viable or desirable solution to energy needs.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities: Criticism of political organizations and figures
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote criticizes Al Gore, a political figure, for his perceived hypocrisy in using large amounts of fossil fuels while advocating for their reduction.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities: Criticism of national governments
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Unless America and all other sovereign nations open up their borders to an illegal alien onslaught, the climate crisis will only continue to worsen, Gore went on to allege.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that national governments, specifically America, are not taking the correct actions to address the climate crisis, and that their policies will exacerbate the problem.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities: Criticism of international entities
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming. This is the goal of the Inflation Reduction Act, a Biden regime initiative that Gore says is 'primarily a climate act.'
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote criticizes international efforts to address climate change, specifically the Inflation Reduction Act, implying that it will have negative consequences for poor people.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote criticizes the broader climate change movement and its predictions, implying that they are unreliable and have a history of being incorrect.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Downplaying climate change: Climate cycles are natural
subnarratives.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true. Global cooling, global warming, mass extinction, mass famine, the polar ice caps melting, mass flooding, the ozone layer depleted, etc.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that climate change is not a significant issue because past predictions have not come true, implying that climate cycles are natural and not affected by human activities.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Downplaying climate change: Temperature increase does not have significant impact
subnarratives.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote downplays the impact of temperature increase by highlighting the supposed failure of past climate predictions, implying that the current warnings are also exaggerated and will not have a significant effect.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Downplaying climate change: Humans and nature will adapt to the changes
subnarratives.evidence_quote: They really are losing their minds, wrote a commenter about how all sorts of common weather events now have scary new names aimed at frightening the general public into giving up cheap and abundant energy in exchange for globalist 'green' energy.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that the concern about climate change is overblown and that humans and nature will adapt to any changes, as common weather events are being given 'scary new names' to frighten the public.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative by criticizing Gore's statement and implying that giving up fossil fuels would have a negative impact, particularly on poor people, which is not directly related to the other subnarratives but still downplays the urgency of addressing climate change.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science: Scientific community is unreliable
subnarratives.evidence_quote: A few years ago, someone (John Nolte?) listed all of their bigger predictions and their track record was 0-47
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that the scientific community, specifically climate scientists, have a history of making incorrect predictions, which undermines their credibility and reliability.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science: Methodologies/metrics used are unreliable/faulty
subnarratives.evidence_quote: They have been losing their minds for decades. I have been hearing about these end of the world scenarios since I was a kid. And not one of their predictions has come true
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that the methodologies and metrics used by climate scientists to make predictions are flawed, as none of their predictions have come true.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Questioning the measurements and science: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Rain bombs? Bomb cyclones? Atmospheric rivers? They really are losing their minds
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that the language and terminology used by climate scientists and activists are exaggerated and misleading, which supports the parent narrative but does not fit into the other specific subnarrative definitions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Climate movement is alarmist
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Climate cultists push bizarre scare language about weather: “rain bombs” and “boiling oceans”
subnarratives.reasoning: The text suggests that the climate movement uses exaggerated and frightening language to describe weather events, implying that they are alarmist and trying to scare the public into accepting their views.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Ad hominem attacks on key activists
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Gore would know about fossil fuel use, seeing as how he uses lots and lots of it – much more than the average person, in fact – to power his lavish, sprawling estates and fuel his private jets.
subnarratives.reasoning: The text attacks Al Gore's personal habits and lifestyle, implying that he is hypocritical and not credible on the issue of climate change, which is a classic ad hominem attack.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Criticism of climate movement: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: “Renewable energy ... is now the cheapest source of electricity in almost the entire planet,” Gore lied.
subnarratives.reasoning: The text implies that Al Gore is dishonest and that the climate movement is promoting false information about renewable energy, which supports the parent narrative but does not fit into the other specific subnarrative definitions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Controversy about green technologies: Renewable energy is costly
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Cheap and abundant oil and natural gas have to go, Gore says – for poor people, anyway – in order for the planet to stop warming.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that transitioning away from fossil fuels to renewable energy will be costly, especially for poor people, implying that renewable energy is not a viable or affordable option.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Controversy about green technologies: Renewable energy is unreliable
subnarratives.evidence_quote: The legislation contains $369 billion, not including tax credits, in appropriation funds for 'renewable energy,' meaning windmills, solar panels, and a very dark and cold future for humanity.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies that renewable energy sources like windmills and solar panels are unreliable and will lead to a 'dark and cold future,' suggesting that they are not dependable for widespread adoption.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: CC: Controversy about green technologies: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: 'Renewable energy ... is now the cheapest source of electricity in almost the entire planet,' Gore lied.
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that Al Gore is being dishonest about the cost of renewable energy, implying that it is not as cheap or efficient as he claims, and that there are other issues with green technologies beyond just cost or reliability.
